questions.question: Which state government launched a special drive to make Holla Mohalla celebrations free from single-use plastic?
questions.explanation: The Punjab Pollution Control Board launches a special drive to make Holla Mohalla celebrations free from single-use plastic. The initiative receives support from the Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee, which manages major gurdwaras. The Jathedar of Sri Akal Takht Sahib appeals to langar committees to replace plastic utensils with steel or biodegradable alternatives. PPCB announces free distribution of biodegradable plates and bowls to langar organisers during the festival. Holla Mohalla takes place from 27 February to 3 March at Anandpur Sahib and Kiratpur Sahib.

questions.question: Jeju Island, that was recently seen in news, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: The Embassy of India in Seoul issues an advisory for Indian nationals travelling to Jeju Island under the visa waiver scheme. Jeju Island is a volcanic island located off the southern coast of South Korea. It is designated as a special autonomous province of South Korea. The island is oval in shape and is the largest as well as the southernmost island of the country. Its provincial capital is Jeju City. The island is formed from volcanic material that rises symmetrically to the summit of Mount Hallasan.

questions.question: What is ‘Impatiens nagorum’ that was recently seen in news?
questions.explanation: Botanists discovered a new flowering plant species named Impatiens nagorum from Northeast India. It was found in Fakim Wildlife Sanctuary in Nagaland. The name “nagorum” honours the Naga tribes of the state. Impatiens, commonly called balsams or “touch-me-nots,” are known for bright flowers and seed pods that burst when touched. The Eastern Himalayas and Northeast India are global hotspots for this plant group. It grows in moist temperate broadleaf forests and is known only from its original discovery site. It has purple flowers, serrated leaves, slightly hairy lateral sepals, and a deep lower sepal forming a hooked spur.

questions.question: National Science Day is celebrated every year on which day?
questions.explanation: National Science Day is celebrated every year on 28 February to honour the contribution of scientists in national development. It marks the discovery of the Raman Effect by Indian physicist C. V. Raman in 1928. For this discovery, he received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930. In 1986, the Government of India declared 28 February as National Science Day. The first celebration was held in 1987 and since then it is observed annually with a special theme. Theme for 202 is “Women in Science: Catalysing Viksit Bharat.”

questions.question: Where was the Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC)-based Digital Food Currency pilot project inaugurated in February 2026?
questions.explanation: The Government of India launched a Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC)-based Digital Food Currency pilot under Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY) in Puducherry on 26th February 2026. The initiative integrates CBDC into the Public Distribution System (PDS) to enhance transparency, efficiency, and beneficiary empowerment, ensuring over 80 crore beneficiaries receive entitlements with clarity and accountability. Under the pilot, programmable digital currency (e₹) tokens generated by Reserve Bank of India are credited directly to beneficiaries’ CBDC wallets.

questions.question: Where was India’s first State Innovation Mission (SIM) launched?
questions.explanation: Atal Innovation Mission under NITI Aayog launched India’s first State Innovation Mission in Tripura. It is part of Atal Innovation Mission 2.0 under State Support Mission framework. It is a long-term institutional system to help States and Union Territories build strong, inclusive and region-specific innovation ecosystems. It aligns national priorities with local strengths and development needs. It will design long-term innovation strategy and improve policy and regulatory support. It promotes partnerships among government, academia, industry and civil society.

questions.question: International Climate Initiative is part of which country’s global climate finance commitment?
questions.explanation: India and Germany launched a Large Grant project worth up to 20 million Euro under the International Climate Initiative. International Climate Initiative is part of Germany’s global climate finance commitment and was established in 2008. It works under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change and Convention on Biological Diversity frameworks. It supports climate mitigation and biodiversity conservation in developing and emerging countries. It helps countries implement Nationally Determined Contributions under the Paris Agreement.

questions.question: Mission Basundhara 3.0, under which land pattas were distributed to over one lakh beneficiaries, was recently launched by which state government?
questions.explanation: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma launched distribution of land pattas under Mission Basundhara 3.0 for over one lakh indigenous landless families and public institutions. The programme was inaugurated at Deuri Beel, Dhemaji district with digital pattas and allotment letters handed to beneficiaries. Mission Basundhara 1.0 resolved conversion and record correction issues for 5.82 lakh families. Mission Basundhara 2.0 granted land rights to indigenous people on government land, benefiting 2.29 lakh families from SC, ST, and OBC communities. Mission Basundhara 4.0 and 5.0 will address remaining land issues, reclassification and settlement of non-tribal forest villagers.

questions.question: RailOne, the SuperApp of Indian Railways, was developed by which organization?
questions.explanation: RailOne, the SuperApp of Indian Railways, crossed two crore downloads within eight months of its launch. The app surpassed five lakh ticket bookings per day in February 2026. It was developed by Centre for Railway Information Systems. It was launched by Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw on 1 July. RailOne integrates services like reserved and unreserved ticketing, platform tickets, train enquiries, PNR status, and other passenger utilities. RailTech Policy promotes innovation by engaging startups, innovators, industry, and institutions for Indian Railways modernization.

questions.question: Which team successfully defended the Team Championship title at the Khelo India Winter Games?
questions.explanation: The Indian Army defended its team championship title at the 6th edition of the Khelo India Winter Games, winning a total of nine gold medals, including eight in the Gulmarg leg. Himachal Pradesh finished as runners-up after securing four gold medals on the final day of competition. The closing ceremony of KIWG 2026 was organised by the Jammu and Kashmir Sports Council and managed by the Sports Authority of India at the Golf Course in Gulmarg. The first leg of the Games was held in Ladakh from January 20–26, 2026.
